diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Complete (not partial) depigmentation with sharp borders is the defining morphology of vitiligo.
Tinea versicolor produces partial hypopigmentation with fine scaling, not stark chalk-white depigmentation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Symmetrical periorificial (mouth/eyes) and acral (backs of hands) distribution

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Symmetrical periorificial and acral involvement is the classic distribution of non-segmental vitiligo.
Tinea versicolor favours the upper trunk and shoulders, not a periorificial/acral pattern.

symptoms.symptom_name: Leukotrichia — a patch of prematurely white hairs within an affected area (eyebrow)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Whitening of hair within affected skin reflects follicular melanocyte involvement and strongly supports vitiligo.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of scaling, flaking, roughness, redness, itch or pain (normal skin texture in the patches)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of fine branny scaling argues against a superficial fungal cause.
Pityriasis alba is mildly scaly with ill-defined borders; sharp, non-scaly, complete depigmentation argues against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal sensation within the patches (no anaesthesia to light touch or temperature)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Tuberculoid leprosy characteristically produces anaesthetic hypopigmented patches; normal sensation is required to exclude it and supports vitiligo. Patient can report sensation, but formal sensory testing is clinician-only and sealed (see SYM-008).

symptoms.symptom_name: Insidious acral onset over months with rapid facial spread over 2 weeks after psychosocial stress

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: An acquired, progressive, sometimes stress-triggered course fits the natural history of non-segmental vitiligo.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute panic attack with hyperventilation and health-anxiety, denying self-harm and chest pain

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hyperventilation, tachycardia and catastrophic fear with preserved speech and no chest pain are characteristic of a reactive panic attack — the acute condition to de-escalate and safety-net.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician skin examination — normal texture/induration in the patches, intact sensory testing, no thickened peripheral nerve, no active border

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal texture with intact sensation and no active border confirms uncomplicated depigmentation. Clinician-only — sealed; the AI Doctor infers this from patient-reported normal sensation and video morphology, not a hands-on exam.
Formal sensory testing and nerve palpation are the definitive discriminators against leprosy; both normal here.

symptoms.symptom_name: Clinician-measured vital signs (BP 135/85, HR 105, RR 22, SpO2 99%, afebrile) reflecting acute anxiety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Mild tachycardia and tachypnoea with normal SpO2 and afebrile are consistent with hyperventilation/anxiety and argue against sepsis or hypoxia. Clinician-measured — sealed; the AI Doctor infers stability from patient-obtainable data, not a measured value.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Baseline autoimmune screen including thyroid function tests (TSH ± thyroid antibodies)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Vitiligo is associated with autoimmune thyroid disease; a baseline screen at routine GP follow-up is appropriate. Not urgent and not required to make the telehealth diagnosis.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Wood's-lamp examination and, if any doubt, skin scraping for KOH microscopy
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Clinician-performed confirmatory tools that accentuate depigmentation and exclude a superficial fungal cause — useful at in-person GP/dermatology review, not needed emergently and not obtainable via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Emergency infective/'flesh-eating' work-up (e.g. urgent bloods, imaging, surgical review)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: There is no infection or necrotising process; investigating the patient's feared 'flesh-eating disease' would be over-investigation reinforcing health anxiety.
